mono.purpose: All body text, captions, metadata. The system's primary voice.

heading-1.purpose: Main page headings. Responsive scale from 30px to 48px.

heading-2.purpose: Section headings. Always uppercase — the system's visual rhythm.

shadows.name: card-hover
shadows.purpose: Hover feedback on interactive cards. Large, diffuse shadow for clear interaction signal.

shadows.name: image-shadow
shadows.purpose: Work card cover images. Lifts visual content from the surface.

motion.name: ease-card-hover
motion.purpose: Card hover shadow transition. 400ms duration for considered, not snappy, feedback.

motion.name: ease-link
motion.purpose: Link color and border transitions. 180ms for responsive but not jarring.

components.name: Card
components.description: Bordered card surface for content grouping. Flat at rest, shadow on hover.
components.html:
Card Title

Card content goes here with a brief description.
components.css: .ds-card { background: var(--colors-warm-stone-surface, #ffffff); color: var(--colors-warm-stone-text, #1c1917); border: 1px solid var(--colors-warm-stone-border, #d6d3d1); border-radius: 6px; padding: 16px; transition: box-shadow 400ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 20px 25px -5px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); } .ds-card-title { font-family: IBM Plex Mono, Courier New, Courier, monospace; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; margin: 0 0 8px 0; } .ds-card-text { font-family: IBM Plex Mono, Courier New, Courier, monospace; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.5; margin: 0; color: var(--colors-warm-stone-muted, #57534e); }

components.name: Inline Link
components.description: Text link with bottom border. Used within content, not for navigation.
components.css: .ds-inline-link { color: var(--colors-warm-stone-text, #1c1917); text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--colors-link-border, #000000); transition: border-color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-inline-link:hover { border-bottom-color: var(--colors-warm-stone-text, #1c1917); }

components.name: Section Heading
components.description: Uppercase section heading. The system's visual rhythm.
components.css: .ds-section-heading { font-family: IBM Plex Mono, Courier New, Courier, monospace; font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.3; text-transform: uppercase; margin: 0 0 16px 0; color: var(--colors-warm-stone-text, #1c1917); }

components.name: Work Card
components.description: Work portfolio card with image and content. Tighter radius than standard cards.
components.html:
Project Title

Project description goes here.
components.css: .ds-work-card-link { display: block; text-decoration: none; color: inherit; transition: box-shadow 500ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-work-card-link:hover { box-shadow: 0 20px 25px -5px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); } .ds-work-card { border: 1px solid var(--colors-warm-stone-border, #d6d3d1); border-radius: 2px; height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column; } .ds-work-card-image { position: relative; width: 100%; padding: 8px 8px 0 8px; overflow: hidden; display: flex; height: 290px; max-height: 290px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--colors-warm-stone-border, #d6d3d1); } .ds-work-card-image img { position: relative; z-index: 10; max-width: 100%; object-fit: cover; object-position: top left; flex-grow: 1; flex-shrink: 0; border-radius: 6px 6px 0 0; box-shadow: 0 10px 15px -3px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); border: 1px solid var(--colors-warm-stone-border, #d6d3d1); border-bottom: none; } .ds-work-card-content { padding: 8px 16px 16px 16px; flex: 1; } .ds-work-card-content h3 { font-family: IBM Plex Mono, Courier New, Courier, monospace; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; margin: 0 0 8px 0; } .ds-work-card-content p { font-family: IBM Plex Mono, Courier New, Courier, monospace; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.5; margin: 0; color: var(--colors-warm-stone-muted, #57534e); }

components.name: Current Job Card
components.description: Featured work card for current role. Amber accent border, spans full width on desktop.
components.html:
Current Role

Description of current work and responsibilities.
components.css: .ds-current-card-link { display: block; text-decoration: none; color: inherit; transition: box-shadow 500ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); grid-column: span 2; } .ds-current-card-link:hover { box-shadow: 0 20px 25px -5px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); } .ds-current-card { border: 2px solid var(--colors-accent, #ffb000); border-radius: 6px; height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column; overflow: hidden; } .ds-current-card-image { width: 100%; height: 192px; overflow: hidden; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--colors-warm-stone-border, #d6d3d1); } .ds-current-card-image img { width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: cover; object-position: top; transition: transform 400ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-current-card-link:hover .ds-current-card-image img { transform: scale(1.05); } .ds-current-card-content { padding: 16px; } .ds-current-card-content h3 { font-family: IBM Plex Mono, Courier New, Courier, monospace; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; margin: 0 0 8px 0; } .ds-current-card-content p { font-family: IBM Plex Mono, Courier New, Courier, monospace; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.5; margin: 0; color: var(--colors-warm-stone-muted, #57534e); }

narrative.overview:
This is a monospace-first design system for a personal portfolio and writing surface. The aesthetic is honest, technical, and clear — a developer's notebook rendered as a website. IBM Plex Mono carries the entire system, from headings to body text, creating a unified technical character that reads as confident rather than constrained.

The system rejects decoration for its own sake. Every element earns its place through function or clarity. Spacing is generous, hierarchy is explicit, and the warm stone neutrals (not cold grays, not cream) ground the interface in something approachable. This is not a terminal cosplay — it's a crafted object that happens to speak the language of code.
keyCharacteristics: Warm stone neutrals over cold grays or saturated backgrounds
Borders over shadows for surface separation
Explicit typographic hierarchy with uppercase H2s
Light/dark theme support with system preference detection

rules.name: The Warmth Rule
rules.body: The light theme background is warm-stone (#f5f5f4), never cool gray, never cream, never saturated beige. The warmth is subtle but non-negotiable — it's what makes the site feel approachable rather than clinical.

rules.name: The Muted-Text Rule
rules.body: Secondary text uses warm-stone-muted (#57534e), never a lighter gray. If the contrast drops below 4.5:1 against the background, bump it darker. Readability over aesthetics.

rules.name: The Amber Rule
rules.body: Amber terminal (#ffb000) is the single accent color. It appears on ≤10% of any given surface — the current job card, the CTA button, interactive highlights. Its rarity is the point. Never use amber for body text or large backgrounds.

rules.name: The Uppercase-H2 Rule
rules.body: Section headings (H2) are always uppercase. This is not optional — it's the system's visual rhythm. If you need a non-uppercase heading, it's an H1 or H3, not an H2.

rules.name: The Monospace-Only Rule
rules.body: IBM Plex Mono is the only active font. VT323 is defined but commented out. Don't introduce new fonts without removing monospace first — the system's identity is monospace-forward.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Cards and surfaces have no shadow at rest. Shadows appear only on hover. If you're tempted to add an ambient shadow 'for depth,' don't — the border is the separator.

rules.name: The Shadow-As-Feedback Rule
rules.body: Shadows are interaction feedback, not decoration. If an element isn't interactive (hoverable, clickable), it doesn't get a shadow. Images are the one exception — they're visual content, not UI surfaces.

dos: Do use IBM Plex Mono for everything. The monospace identity is non-negotiable.
Do make H2 headings uppercase. This is the system's visual rhythm.
Do use warm-stone-bg (#f5f5f4) as the page background. Never cool gray, never cream.
Do use borders for surface separation. Cards have 1px borders at rest, not shadows.
Do keep content max-width at 768px. This is the reading measure — don't exceed it for prose.
Do use shadows only as hover feedback. Cards get shadow on hover, not at rest.
Do maintain 4.5:1 contrast ratio for body text. warm-stone-muted (#57534e) on warm-stone-bg (#f5f5f4) is the minimum.
Do use amber terminal (#ffb000) as the single accent color. Use it on ≤10% of any surface — the current job card, CTA buttons, interactive highlights.
donts: Don't use generic AI-generated portfolio patterns: cream/beige backgrounds, gradient text, eyebrow+kicker patterns, identical card grids. If it looks like every AI output of 2025-2026, it's wrong.
Don't use corporate LinkedIn energy: stock-photo vibes, buzzword-heavy, template layouts, safe-to-the-point-of-bland.
Don't use over-designed SaaS landing pages: glassmorphism, hero metrics, feature grids, dark-mode-by-default-for-coolness. This is a person, not a product launch.
Don't introduce new fonts without removing monospace first. The system's identity is monospace-forward.
Don't add ambient shadows to non-interactive surfaces. Shadows are hover feedback, not decoration.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent. Side-stripe borders are prohibited.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text with gradient background). Use solid colors.
Don't use glassmorphism as default. Blurs and glass cards are rare and purposeful, or nothing.
Don't use the hero-metric template (big number, small label, supporting stats, gradient accent). This is a portfolio, not a SaaS dashboard.
Don't use identical card grids (same-sized cards with icon + heading + text, repeated endlessly). Vary the layout.
Don't use tiny uppercase tracked eyebrows above every section. One named kicker as a deliberate brand system is voice; an eyebrow on every section is AI grammar.
